description:
Choropleth map displaying the spatial distribution of unquantified values across European sub-national regions, with country borders outlined in red. The map covers EU Member States, the United Kingdom, Ireland, Iceland, Norway, Switzerland, the Western Balkans, and Turkey. A graduated colour legend uses five shades of green to represent value ranges:
*   Lightest green: 0.2 - 1.8
*   Second lightest green: 1.8 - 2.9
*   Mid-green: 2.9 - 4.3
*   Second darkest green: 4.3 - 6.5
*   Darkest green: 6.5 - 9.4

Regions with the highest values (6.5 - 9.4, darkest green) include most of Ireland, western France, and parts of Great Britain. Regions showing the lowest values (0.2 - 1.8, lightest green) are widespread across the Iberian Peninsula (Spain, Portugal), Greece, Finland, most of Sweden, the Baltic states, and regions in Eastern Europe (e.g., Poland, Romania, Bulgaria, Turkey). Intermediate values (1.8 - 6.5) are observed across central Europe, including Germany, Italy, Austria, and remaining parts of the Nordic countries. No specific units, data source, or reference year are visible on the map.
